agent_output: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"SQL injection vulnerability in public-facing endpoint — direct string interpolation of user input into raw SQL."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized query parameters","risk":"Attacker can exfiltrate, modify, or destroy the entire database by injecting SQL through customer_id or status_filter.","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries function, lines 2-6 (f-string query construction)","reproduction":"GET /deliveries?customer_id=' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --&status_filter=delivered — the f-string interpolates the payload directly into the WHERE clause, producing: WHERE customer_id = '' OR 1=1; DROP TABLE deliveries; --' AND status = 'delivered'","fix":"Use parameterized queries: query = 'SELECT * FROM deliveries WHERE customer_id = ? AND status = ?'; result = db_conn.execute(query, (customer_id, status_filter))","time_to_fix":"5 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Security — any public-facing endpoint must be immune to injection attacks"},{"title":"No input validation on public API parameters","risk":"Even with parameterized queries, missing validation allows unbounded or nonsensical queries (e.g., empty customer_id returns nothing silently, garbage status_filter wastes DB resources).","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function signature / entry point","reproduction":"GET /deliveries?customer_id=&status_filter=anything — no check that customer_id is non-empty or that status_filter is a valid enum value.","fix":"Validate customer_id is non-empty and matches expected format (e.g., UUID regex). Validate status_filter against an allow-list of known statuses (e.g., 'pending', 'delivered', 'cancelled'). Return 400 for invalid input.","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"API correctness and abuse prevention"},{"title":"SELECT * exposes all columns including potentially sensitive data","risk":"Returning all columns may leak internal fields (internal_notes, driver_phone, cost_price, PII) to the public API consumer. Schema changes silently expand the attack surface.","severity":"High","location":"search_deliveries function, SQL query","reproduction":"Add a sensitive column to the deliveries table — it is automatically returned to the public API without any review gate.","fix":"Explicitly enumerate the columns needed: SELECT id, customer_id, status, estimated_delivery, tracking_number FROM deliveries. Map to a response DTO before returning.","time_to_fix":"10 minutes","acceptance_criteria_affected":"Data privacy / least-privilege data exposure"}],"important_issues":[{"title":"No pagination or result limit","description":"A customer with thousands of deliveries or a broad status filter will return an unbounded result set, risking OOM on the app server and heavy DB load. Add LIMIT/OFFSET or cursor-based pagination.","location":"search_deliveries SQL query"},{"title":"No authorization check — any caller can query any customer_id","description":"The function trusts the caller-supplied customer_id. A logged-in user can enumerate other customers' deliveries by changing the parameter. The endpoint must verify the authenticated user owns the requested customer_id.","location":"search_deliveries function entry"}],"suggestions":["Add an index on (customer_id, status) if one does not already exist — this query will be called on every search.","Return a typed dataclass or Pydantic model instead of raw Row tuples to prevent leaking internal schema changes.","Add query timeout to prevent long-running queries from degrading the connection pool."],"production_readiness":{"deployment_risks":"This code must NOT ship — it is trivially exploitable from the public internet.","rollback_capability":"N/A — do not deploy.","backward_compatibility":"Switching to parameterized queries is backward-compatible for all legitimate callers.","monitoring_coverage":"No logging or metrics on this endpoint. Add request logging and alert on unusual query patterns post-fix.","breaking_changes":{"api_breaking":false,"db_destructive":false,"shared_library":false,"auth_security":true,"data_pipeline":false,"config_changes":false,"details":["auth_security: no authorization check means any authenticated (or unauthenticated) user can access any customer's delivery data"]},"safe_to_deploy":false,"rollback_plan":"Do not deploy. Fix the injection vulnerability, add input validation and authorization, then re-review."},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":99,"high_confidence_areas":["SQL injection is textbook-certain — f-string interpolation of user input into SQL","Missing input validation is directly observable","SELECT * risk is directly observable"],"low_confidence_areas":["Authorization gap assumes no middleware check exists upstream — if a gateway enforces customer_id scoping, the severity drops from Critical to Important"],"assumptions":["No ORM or middleware sanitizes inputs before this function is called","customer_id and status_filter arrive as raw strings from HTTP query parameters as stated"]}}
